description: You are a swarm robotics researcher preparing a multi-robot simulation for a coordinated coverage experiment. Audit the loaded Webots world and fix all configuration issues preventing the robots from executing behaviors. Ensure the simulation is ready for launch by correctly configuring the robot controllers based on the Webots soccer demo environment. Save the corrected world to /home/ga/Desktop/swarm_ready.wbt.

natural_language.prompt:
You are a swarm robotics researcher. A multi-robot Webots world has been loaded for a coordinated experiment, but it has configuration problems that prevent the experiment from running correctly.

Your tasks:
1. Examine each robot in the scene tree. Identify what is wrong with the robot configurations.
2. Fix the robot controllers so each robot can actually execute behavior (the robots are from the Webots soccer demo — use the controllers available in the world's controllers directory).
3. Check the simulation physics settings — there may be additional problems with the world configuration.
4. Ensure robots are in valid, non-overlapping positions so the simulation can run without immediate collision errors.

Save the fixed world to /home/ga/Desktop/swarm_ready.wbt.

planted_errors: All soccer player robots have controller set to 'soccer_player_broken' (non-existent controller)
All soccer player robots have same translation position (overlapping robots)
WorldInfo basicTimeStep set to 128 (too slow for soccer robot control frequency)
metadata.occupation: Swarm Robotics Researcher / Multi-Robot Systems Engineer
